subject: Databases
title: Hot Row Contention
description: A short lesson to help you apply Hot Row Contention.

hook.text: A global counter row is updated by every request. 10,000 requests per second, all waiting for one lock.

buildup.text: Hot rows are single rows that many transactions compete to update. They serialize all concurrent access.

discovery.text: Fix: use sharded counters. Split one counter into 10 rows, each incrementing independently. Sum them when reading.

twist.text: Queue tables where every worker pops row 1 have the same issue. SKIP LOCKED lets workers grab different rows.

climax.text: Identify hot rows with pg_stat_activity or lock monitoring. If many transactions wait on one row, it's hot.

punchline.text: Hot rows bottleneck concurrency. Shard or skip locked rows to spread load.

quiz.question: How do sharded counters reduce hot row contention?
options: They split one counter into multiple rows so concurrent updates don't all compete for the same lock
They eliminate the need for transactions entirely
Sharded counters store data in memory instead of disk
